architecture.evidence: Three-layer architecture: pipeline_lib (data) → scripts (CLI) → pipeline_api (programmatic) → mcp_server (AI). Clean information flow. Module decomposition is systematic: pipeline_lib extracted into 3 sub-modules (entry_state, freshness, market). Standup similarly decomposed (constants, work_sections, relationship_sections). Score system uses separate constants, dimensions modules. The DAG property holds — no circular imports. The state machine is formalized in pipeline_entry_state.py with explicit VALID_TRANSITIONS.
strengths: Information flow is strictly layered: data → CLI → API → MCP
State machine formalization prevents ad-hoc status transitions
Module extraction follows natural domain boundaries (freshness is separate from state)
weaknesses: pipeline_lib still re-exports from sub-modules for backward compat — conceptual overhead
No dependency injection; scripts import modules directly, making isolated testing harder
Scoring rubric YAML and score.py _DEFAULT_WEIGHTS are duplicated (audit catches drift but shouldn't exist)

documentation.evidence: CLAUDE.md is a reference-quality onboarding document: state machine, identity positions, content composition model, script dependency graph, module architecture table, configuration files, CI & linting, testing patterns. docs/ contains architecture.md, workflow.md, SOPs. Scoring rubric documented in both YAML and scoring-rubric.md. Market intelligence has 126-source annotated bibliography. The system is self-documenting through the diagnostic tool.
strengths: CLAUDE.md serves as both onboarding doc and architectural specification
Market research corpus is a model for evidence-based documentation
weaknesses: Architecture decisions not captured in ADR format
Some workflow sequences only documented in CLAUDE.md, not in dedicated docs

analytics_intelligence.evidence: Strong multi-dimensional scoring: 9 dimensions with 2 weight sets adapting to job vs general tracks. Conversion funnel tracks stage→stage rates. Snapshot system provides trend detection with regression slopes. Rejection learner and block-outcome correlation provide feedback loops. Org intelligence aggregates cross-entry signals. Market intelligence integrates 112 sources. The scoring model lacks outcome validation — claims like '8x referral multiplier' aren't yet validated by this pipeline's own data.
strengths: Multi-dimensional scoring with domain-specific weights is architecturally sound
Feedback loops (rejection learner, block outcomes) enable system learning
Market intelligence integration provides external calibration
weaknesses: Scoring model claims are based on external benchmarks, not validated against own outcomes
Text matching uses TF-IDF — architecturally simpler than embedding similarity
No A/B testing framework for variant effectiveness measurement

sustainability.evidence: Strong automation layer: 7 launchd agents, agent.py for autonomous actions, notify.py for event dispatch. CLAUDE.md provides sufficient context for any AI model to operate the system. The three-layer API architecture (pipeline_api.py → cli.py → mcp_server.py) means the system can be operated programmatically, via CLI, or via MCP. SOP documentation started. The diagnostic tool itself demonstrates sustainability — the system grades itself.
strengths: Multi-interface design (CLI, API, MCP) maximizes operator flexibility
Self-diagnostic capability reduces dependency on original author
Automation covers the full daily cadence (morning → evening)
weaknesses: Single operator model — no delegation or team features
Scaling beyond single-operator use would require significant redesign
